signals.headline: Trump Announces Tariffs on 8 European Countries if Greenland Deal Fails | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Announces Tariffs on 8 European Countries if Greenland Deal Fails | YES | HIGH confidence**  
President Trump escalated his push for U.S. control of Greenland, announcing on Saturday that Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the UK, the Netherlands, and Finland face 10% tariffs starting February 1, 2026, rising to 25% by June 1 if no deal is reached; this triggered market reactions incl

signals.headline: Gold Hits New Record High Amid Geopolitical Tensions from Trump’s Greenland Threats | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Gold Hits New Record High Amid Geopolitical Tensions from Trump’s Greenland Threats | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Gold surged to a new record, jumping over 4% last week post-Maduro capture and further on Monday due to fears of NATO rift, global order disruption, and dollar damage from Trump’s Greenland ambitions, with European defense stocks also at all-time highs.[1][2]

signals.headline: Trump Signals Imminent Davos Meetings on Greenland Acquisition | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Trump Signals Imminent Davos Meetings on Greenland Acquisition | YES | MED confidence**  
Trump stated things "will work out very well" on Greenland with multiple meetings lined up over the next few days at Davos, amid his willingness to pursue acquisition aggressively.[1]
